summary: The review of the "Service Provider Agreement Example.pdf" indicates a moderate risk posture, with several amber and red flags requiring attention. Key issues include overly broad indemnity obligations (red flag, POS-08), a disrepute clause that fails to preserve academic freedoms (red flag, POS-20), and gaps in confidentiality, intellectual property, termination, and taxation clauses (amber flags, POS-11, POS-IP, POS-19, POS-06). The next step is to escalate the flagged clauses to the appropriate UoA teams, including Research Contract Delegations, Auckland UniServices Ltd, and the Tax and Compliance team, for further review and alignment with UoA's contracting positions.

flags.clause_title: (c) all other liabilities and expenses of whatever nature relating to the Servic
flags.snippet: (c) all other liabilities and expenses of whatever nature relating to the Service Provider. The Service Provider indemnifies the Company against all such taxes, levies, insurance premiums and other expenses.
flags.rationale: The clause imposes an indemnity obligation on the Service Provider for 'all other liabilities and expenses of whatever nature,' which is overly broad and undefined. This conflicts with POS-08 (Indemnities), which explicitly states that indemnities should only cover damage/injury arising from negligent acts/omissions or wilful misconduct of the University, and must include proportionate reduction for contributory negligence and obligations to mitigate losses. Additionally, indemnities against taxes, levies, and insurance premiums are not addressed in the UoA Positions, making this clause incompatible with UoA's preferred contracting stance. Escalation to Research Contract Delegations is required.

flags.clause_title: (f) not do anything to prejudice the good name of the Company in any dealings wi
flags.snippet: (f) not do anything to prejudice the good name of the Company in any dealings with third parties including clients/customers;
flags.rationale: This clause falls under the topic of 'No Disrepute Clauses' (POS-20). The UoA Preferred Contracting Position explicitly states that clauses precluding UoA academic staff or students from saying or doing anything that would bring the client into 'disrepute' are not acceptable unless they include wording preserving academic freedoms under s267 of the Education and Training Act 2020 (NZ). This clause does not include such wording, and therefore materially deviates from the Preferred Position. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts is required.

flags.clause_title: 2.1 The Service Provider’s appointment will commence on the date specified in It
flags.snippet: 2.1 The Service Provider’s appointment will commence on the date specified in Item 6 of the Schedule and, subject to clauses 2.6 and 9, will terminate on the date specified in Item 7 of the Schedule without the need for either party to give the other party any notice.
flags.rationale: This clause relates to the topic of Termination, which is a UoA-sensitive area. The clause specifies automatic termination without notice, but it does not address mutual two-way termination for material breach, insolvency, or UoA's right to terminate for non-payment. It also does not clarify whether accrued rights and benefits are preserved or whether termination costs (including uncancellable obligations) are covered. While it does not explicitly contradict UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions, it lacks sufficient detail to confirm alignment. This requires further review to ensure compliance with UoA Position #POS-19.

flags.clause_title: 2.3 The parties agree that the Service Provider’s Key Personnel defined in Item
flags.snippet: 2.3 The parties agree that the Service Provider’s Key Personnel defined in Item 10 of the Schedule, are the person(s) to provide the Services on behalf of the Service Provider. Any variation to the Service Provider’s Ke…
flags.rationale: This clause touches on termination rights, which are sensitive under UoA Position #POS-19. While the clause allows termination if replacement Key Personnel cannot be agreed upon within 30 days, it does not align with the Preferred Position of mutual two-way termination for material breach or insolvency, nor does it explicitly address accrued rights or termination costs. However, it does not fall outside the Acceptable tier, as termination for convenience is permissible under certain conditions for Consulting contracts. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts, is recommended to confirm alignment with UoA's termination policy.

flags.clause_title: 4.1 The Company will pay the Service Provider for the Services the fees and expe
flags.snippet: 4.1 The Company will pay the Service Provider for the Services the fees and expenses set out in Item 5 of the Schedule at the times and in the manner set out in clause 6 and Item 5 of the Schedule on receipt of an itemised GST invoice from the Service Provider. The Company shall make all necessary withholding tax deductions from the charges payable to the Service Provider unless this is not required, or the Service Provider supplies the Company with a Certificate of Exemption.
flags.rationale: This clause touches on financial matters, including payment terms and taxation, which are UoA-sensitive topics. While the clause mentions GST invoices and withholding tax, it does not explicitly align with UoA's preferred position under POS-06 ('NZ contracts: costs presented as 'excluding GST'. Overseas contracts: costs 'excluding any applicable taxes and duties, payable by the Client on any taxable supplies'). Additionally, the clause does not address liability limitations or exclusions directly, but the reference to 'necessary withholding tax deductions' could have implications for financial liability. This requires further review to ensure compliance with UoA's financial and liability policies.

flags.clause_title: (a) its own liability for all taxes, payments, and levies under any relevant leg
flags.snippet: (a) its own liability for all taxes, payments, and levies under any relevant legislation including the Income Tax Act 2007 and all accident compensation legislation;
flags.rationale: The clause addresses taxation, which is a UoA-sensitive topic under Position #POS-06. While it does not conflict with the preferred position ('costs presented as excluding GST' for NZ contracts or 'excluding any applicable taxes and duties' for overseas contracts), it does not explicitly align with the preferred or acceptable tier. The clause is ambiguous regarding whether the tax liability is limited to the contracting party or extends to UoA. This requires confirmation by the Contract Manager to ensure compliance with UoA's taxation policy. Escalation to the Tax and Compliance team may be necessary if offshore tax incorporation is required.

flags.clause_title: (b) registering for GST and complying with the Goods and Services Tax Act 1985 a
flags.snippet: (b) registering for GST and complying with the Goods and Services Tax Act 1985 and all GST requirements;
flags.rationale: The clause addresses GST compliance, which falls under the UoA Position #POS-06 on Taxation. While the clause mentions compliance with the Goods and Services Tax Act 1985, it does not explicitly align with the Preferred Position, which requires costs to be presented as 'excluding GST' for NZ contracts or 'excluding any applicable taxes and duties, payable by the Client on any taxable supplies' for overseas contracts. The clause does not contradict the Position but lacks the specificity required for a green flag. Escalation to the Tax and Compliance team is recommended if offshore tax incorporation is required.

flags.clause_title: Confidential Information of a party (Disclosing Party) means all information tha
flags.snippet: Confidential Information of a party (Disclosing Party) means all information that is about the Disclosing Party, its Affiliates, or its or its Affiliates’ affairs, or obtained by the other party (Recipient) directly or indirectly from the Disclosing Party or its Affiliates in connection with this Agreement, and includes the terms and conditions of this Agreement, but excludes information:
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the definition of Confidential Information but does not explicitly exclude information already public, independently developed, previously known by the receiving party, or legally required to be disclosed, as required by the UoA Preferred Position. Additionally, the clause does not specify the ability for UoA to disclose to agents and Affiliates or the duration of confidentiality obligations (up to 5 years post-completion). These omissions place the clause in the amber tier, requiring confirmation or amendment to align with UoA Position #POS-11.

flags.clause_title: Improvement means any improvement, development, enhancement, modification or
flags.snippet: Improvement means any improvement, development, enhancement, modification or derivative of any product, design, process or Intellectual Property Right that cannot be used independently of such product, design, process o…
flags.rationale: The clause pertains to Intellectual Property, specifically defining 'Improvement' in relation to IP rights. UoA Contracting Positions explicitly state that all IP-related clauses must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd (Head of IP) for review, as IP positions are not governed by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions. This clause falls under the 'acceptable' tier by default, pending UniServices review.

flags.clause_title: Intellectual Property Rights means all intellectual property rights conferred by
flags.snippet: Intellectual Property Rights means all intellectual property rights conferred by law and includes the rights to, and any interests in, any patent, trade mark, service mark, copyright, moral right, trade name, brand name…
flags.rationale: Intellectual Property — UoA Contracting Positions does not cover IP. All IP clauses must be referred to Auckland UniServices (Head of IP) for review.

flags.clause_title: Pre-existing IP means the Intellectual Property Rights that a party makes availa
flags.snippet: Pre-existing IP means the Intellectual Property Rights that a party makes available to the other party under this Agreement which existed prior to the Commencement Date or which were created independently to the perform…
flags.rationale: The clause pertains to Intellectual Property, specifically defining Pre-existing IP and including Improvements. UoA Contracting Positions explicitly state that all IP clauses must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd (Head of IP) for review, as IP positions are not governed by the UoA protocol. This clause falls under the Acceptable tier for IP clauses, requiring referral.

flags.clause_title: 1. Interpretation
flags.snippet: 1. Interpretation
flags.rationale: The clause addresses 'Interpretation,' which is not covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or associated templates. As such, it falls outside the scope of the policy framework.

flags.clause_title: 1.1 In this Agreement, unless the context otherwise requires:
flags.snippet: 1.1 In this Agreement, unless the context otherwise requires:
flags.rationale: The clause is a general interpretative provision and does not fall under any specific topic addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions. It is standard legal boilerplate and does not conflict with UoA policies.

flags.clause_title: 1.2 In this Agreement:
flags.snippet: 1.2 In this Agreement:
flags.rationale: The clause appears to be part of a definitions or interpretation section, which is not specifically addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented positions and no template exists for this contract type.

flags.clause_title: 2. Appointment and term
flags.snippet: 2. Appointment and term
flags.rationale: The topic of 'Appointment and term' is not addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions, and there is no UoA Standard Template for this contract type. As such, this clause falls outside the scope of the documented positions.

flags.clause_title: 2.2 The Service Provider will be responsible for:
flags.snippet: 2.2 The Service Provider will be responsible for:
flags.rationale: The clause topic is not addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions document, and there is no UoA Standard Template for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of predefined positions and requires independent review.

flags.clause_title: 2.4 The Service Provider will perform the Services and will not incur any obliga
flags.snippet: 2.4 The Service Provider will perform the Services and will not incur any obligations on behalf of the Company without the Companys prior written consent.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the Service Provider's obligation not to incur obligations on behalf of the Company without prior written consent. This topic is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or any registered UoA Standard Template for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of the policy framework and is flagged as blue.

flags.clause_title: 2.5 The parties acknowledge that the Service Provider is not in partnership, joi
flags.snippet: 2.5 The parties acknowledge that the Service Provider is not in partnership, joint venture, or integrated into the business of the Company and is in business on the Service Provider’s own account.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the relationship between the parties (e.g., not a partnership or joint venture), which is not covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or associated templates. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented policy framework.

flags.clause_title: 2.6 If the parties agree that the Service Provider’s appointment will continue a
flags.snippet: 2.6 If the parties agree that the Service Provider’s appointment will continue after the date specified in Item 7 of the Schedule, both parties’ authorised representatives defined in Item 2 and Item 3 should be notified.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses a procedural matter regarding notification of authorised representatives if the Service Provider's appointment is extended. This topic is not covered in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or associated escalation protocols, and no UoA Standard Template exists for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of the defined policy framework.

flags.clause_title: 3. Obligations of the Service Provider
flags.snippet: 3. Obligations of the Service Provider
flags.rationale: The clause addresses 'Obligations of the Service Provider,' which is not explicitly covered in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or associated templates. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented positions.

flags.clause_title: 3.1 The Service Provider will:
flags.snippet: 3.1 The Service Provider will:
flags.rationale: The clause is introductory and does not contain substantive content. It is not addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions, and no UoA Standard Template exists for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of the Positions document.

flags.clause_title: 3.2 The Service Provider warrants that the Services will meet the quality standa
flags.snippet: 3.2 The Service Provider warrants that the Services will meet the quality standards and specifications set out in Item 4 of the Schedule, within the timetable (if any) set out in Item 4 of the Schedule.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses warranties related to service quality and adherence to specifications, but this specific topic is not covered in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions document. There is no applicable standard template for this contract type, and no escalation route is defined for this topic.

flags.clause_title: 3.3 The Service Provider agrees that this Agreement does not in any way prevent,
flags.snippet: 3.3 The Service Provider agrees that this Agreement does not in any way prevent, restrict or limit the Company from engaging other persons to provide the same or similar services and that no claim shall like against the Company should the Company exercise such rights.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the Company's ability to engage other service providers for similar services, which is not covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the UoA Standard Template. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented policies.

flags.clause_title: 4. Obligations of the Company
flags.snippet: 4. Obligations of the Company
flags.rationale: The clause topic 'Obligations of the Company' is not addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions, and there is no UoA Standard Template for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of the defined policy framework.

flags.clause_title: (a) clause and other headings are for ease of reference only and will not be dee
flags.snippet: (a) clause and other headings are for ease of reference only and will not be deemed to form any part of the context or to affect the interpretation of this Agreement; and
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the interpretation of headings within the agreement, which is not covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or associated policies. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented positions.

flags.clause_title: (b) words importing the singular include the plural and vice versa.
flags.snippet: (b) words importing the singular include the plural and vice versa.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses a general interpretative rule (singular/plural usage) that is not covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or associated templates. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented policy framework.

flags.clause_title: (b) provide the Company with such information, and prepare such reports, in rela
flags.snippet: (b) provide the Company with such information, and prepare such reports, in relation to the Services as the Company may reasonably require as outlined in the Schedule;
flags.rationale: The clause pertains to the provision of information and preparation of reports, which is not explicitly addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions. As there is no applicable policy or template reference, the clause falls outside the scope of the documented positions.

flags.clause_title: (c) give priority to the Company’s requirements in the carrying out of the Servi
flags.snippet: (c) give priority to the Company’s requirements in the carrying out of the Services;
flags.rationale: The clause requires the University to give priority to the Company's requirements in performing the services. This topic is not addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions, and there is no UoA Standard Template for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented positions and is flagged as blue.

flags.clause_title: (d) may, with the prior written consent of the Company, engage a nominated sub-
flags.snippet: (d) may, with the prior written consent of the Company, engage a nominated sub- Service Provider provided they are suitably skilled to perform the Services and are beforehand approved by the Company to carry out any part of the Services but, in such event, will take full responsibility for all aspects of the sub-Service Provider’s performance, remuneration and compliance with the terms of this agreement and of any relevant legislation or regulations;
flags.rationale: The clause addresses subcontracting, which is not covered by any UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or templates for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented policy and is flagged as blue.

flags.clause_title: (e) comply with the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015, and any health and safet
flags.snippet: (e) comply with the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015, and any health and safety procedures of the Company and of any site where the Services are being carried out;
flags.rationale: The clause addresses compliance with health and safety laws and procedures, which is not covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or any registered UoA Standard Template for this contract type. As such, it falls outside the scope of the documented positions and is flagged as blue.

flags.clause_title: (g) at its own cost, make good any errors or omissions in the Services of which
flags.snippet: (g) at its own cost, make good any errors or omissions in the Services of which the Company notifies the Service Provider in writing during the term of this Agreement; and
flags.rationale: The clause requires the Service Provider to rectify errors or omissions at its own cost during the term of the Agreement. This topic is not explicitly addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions, and there is no UoA Standard Template for this contract type to provide guidance.

flags.clause_title: (h) notify the Company of any interest the Service Provider may have which confl
flags.snippet: (h) notify the Company of any interest the Service Provider may have which conflicts with the Company’s interests immediately on becoming aware of such conflict.
flags.rationale: The topic of conflict of interest notification is not addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or any registered UoA Standard Template for this contract type. As such, this clause falls outside the scope of the documented positions and is flagged as blue.

flags.clause_title: Agreement means this Service Provider Agreement including the Schedule.
flags.snippet: Agreement means this Service Provider Agreement including the Schedule.
flags.rationale: The clause defines the term 'Agreement' but does not address any topic covered by the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions. As there is no applicable policy or template for this definition, the flag remains blue.

flags.clause_title: Algorithm means a set of code created under the Services for an inventory
flags.snippet: Algorithm means a set of code created under the Services for an inventory management solution.
flags.rationale: The clause defines 'Algorithm' in the context of the services provided, which is not a topic addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions. There is no specific guidance or standard template for this type of definition.

flags.clause_title: Data Outputs means the output of the Algorithm in the form of lists of raw produ
flags.snippet: Data Outputs means the output of the Algorithm in the form of lists of raw product recommendations (inventory choices) for an iteration of product orders.
flags.rationale: The clause defines 'Data Outputs' as the output of an algorithm, which is a topic not addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions. No applicable standard or template exists for this contract type.

flags.clause_title: Documentation means details of the algorithm processes (but not the code) and
flags.snippet: Documentation means details of the algorithm processes (but not the code) and processing environment.
flags.rationale: The clause defines 'Documentation' in terms of algorithm processes and processing environment, which is not addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or tied to any specific policy. No escalation or pre-approved deviation applies.

flags.clause_title: Services means the services to be performed by the Service Provider as described
flags.snippet: Services means the services to be performed by the Service Provider as described in Item 4 of the Schedule; and
flags.rationale: The clause defines the scope of services to be performed by the Service Provider, which is a standard definitional clause. This topic is not addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions, and there is no UoA Standard Template for this contract type. As such, the clause falls outside the scope of the Positions document.

flags.clause_title: This Agreement is made on the date set out in Item 1 of the Schedule
flags.snippet: This Agreement is made on the date set out in Item 1 of the Schedule Between (1) The company whose name appears in Item 2 of the Schedule (the Company) And The University of Auckland (2) The company whose name appears i…
flags.rationale: The clause primarily addresses introductory formalities and the appointment of parties, which is not covered by any specific UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or template. It does not conflict with any established UoA policies and is therefore flagged as blue.

flags.clause_title: Working Day means a day on which businesses and banks in Auckland are generally
flags.snippet: Working Day means a day on which businesses and banks in Auckland are generally open for business.
flags.rationale: The definition of 'Working Day' is not addressed in the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions, and there is no UoA Standard Template for this contract type. As such, this clause falls outside the scope of the documented positions.

flags.clause_title: (a) which is or becomes public knowledge, other than as a result of any fault of
flags.snippet: (a) which is or becomes public knowledge, other than as a result of any fault of the Recipient or its permitted disclosees;
flags.rationale: This clause pertains to the definition of confidential information, which is addressed under UoA Position POS-11. The clause matches the preferred tier, as it excludes information already public from confidentiality obligations, aligning with the UoA's standard approach.

flags.clause_title: (b) developed independently of this Agreement and any information of the
flags.snippet: (b) developed independently of this Agreement and any information of the Disclosing Party; or received by the Recipient from a third party without restriction and without any breach of any obligation of confidentiality.
flags.rationale: The clause aligns with UoA Position #POS-11 on Confidentiality. It excludes information independently developed or received from a third party without restriction, which matches the Preferred tier definition of confidential information. The clause does not conflict with UoA's ability to disclose to agents and Affiliates, nor does it impose unreasonable confidentiality obligations.

flags.clause_title: (a) perform the Services on the terms set out in this Agreement with due care, s
flags.snippet: (a) perform the Services on the terms set out in this Agreement with due care, skill and diligence and in a timely and professional manner;
flags.rationale: The clause aligns with UoA Position POS-10 on Warranties, which states that the University warrants performance to a professional standard and usual good practice. This clause matches the preferred tier as it specifies performance with due care, skill, diligence, and professionalism.
